ibm.cloud.ibm_cm_catalog.description: This module creates, updates, C(resource_alias) or deletes a ibm_cm_catalog. By default the module will look for an existing ibm_cm_catalog.

resource_group_id.description: Resource group id the catalog is owned by.

kind.description: Kind of catalog. Supported kinds are offering and vpe.

catalog_icon_url.description: URL for an icon associated with this catalog.

label.description: Display Name in the requested language.

tags.description: List of tags associated with this catalog.

features.description: List of features associated with this catalog.

disabled.description: Denotes whether a catalog is disabled.

catalog_filters.description: Filters for account and catalog filters.

state.description: Should the resource be present or absent.

ibm.cloud.ibm_cm_offering.description: This module creates, updates, or deletes a ibm_cm_offering. By default the module will look for an existing ibm_cm_offering.

short_description.description: Short description in the requested language.

offering_support_url.description:
[deprecated] Use offering.support instead.
URL to be displayed in the Consumption UI for getting support on this offering.

metadata.description: Map of metadata values for this offering.

keywords.description: List of keywords associated with offering, typically used to search for it.

hidden.description: Determine if this offering should be displayed in the Consumption UI.

public_publish_approved.description: Indicates if this offering has been approved for use by all IBM Cloud users.

media.description: A list of media items related to this offering.

share_with_ibm.description: Denotes IBM employee availability of an Offering.

features.description: list of features associated with this offering.

ibm_publish_approved.description: Indicates if this offering has been approved for use by all IBMers.

public_original_crn.description: The original offering CRN that this publish entry came from.

share_with_all.description: Denotes public availability of an Offering.

offering_docs_url.description: URL for an additional docs with this offering.

crn.description: The crn for this specific offering.

pc_managed.description: Offering is managed by Partner Center.

share_enabled.description: Denotes access list availability of an Offering.

created.description: The date and time this catalog was created.

publish_approved.description: Offering has been approved to publish to permitted to IBM or Public Catalog.

offering_icon_url.description: URL for an icon associated with this offering.

label.description: Display Name in the requested language.

long_description.description: Long description in the requested language.

url.description: The url for this specific offering.

tags.description: List of tags associated with this catalog.

catalog_id.description: The id of the catalog containing this offering.

permit_request_ibm_public_publish.description: Is it permitted to request publishing to IBM or Public.

name.description: The programmatic name of this offering.

publish_public_crn.description: The crn of the public catalog entry of this offering.

updated.description: The date and time this catalog was last updated.

provider_info.description: Information on the provider for this offering, or omitted if no provider information is given.

digest.description: Return the digest format of the specified offering.  Default is false.

type.description: Offering Parameter Type.  Valid values are 'name' or 'id'.  Default is 'id'.

state.description: Should the resource be present or absent.

ibm.cloud.ibm_cm_offering_instance.description: This module creates, updates, or deletes a ibm_cm_offering_instance. By default the module will look for an existing ibm_cm_offering_instance.

kind_format.description: the format this instance has (helm, operator, ova...).

install_plan.description:
Type of install plan (also known as approval strategy) for operator subscriptions.
Can be either automatic, which automatically upgrades operators to the latest in a channel,
or manual, which requires approval on the cluster.

metadata.description: Map of metadata values for this offering instance.

resource_group_id.description: Id of the resource group to provision the offering instance into.

channel.description: Channel to pin the operator subscription to.

offering_id.description: Offering ID this instance was created from.

version.description: The version this instance was installed from (not version id).

catalog_id.description: Catalog ID this instance was created from.

schematics_workspace_id.description: Id of the schematics workspace, for offering instances provisioned through schematics.

id.description: provisioned instance ID (part of the CRN).

cluster_namespaces.description: List of target namespaces to install into.

cluster_all_namespaces.description: designate to install into all namespaces.

last_operation.description: the last operation performed and status.

state.description: Should the resource be present or absent.

ibm.cloud.ibm_cm_version.description: This module creates, updates, or deletes a ibm_cm_version. By default the module will look for an existing ibm_cm_version.

content.description: byte array representing the content to be imported.  Only supported for OVA images at this time.

target_kinds.description: Target kinds.  Current valid values are 'iks', 'roks', 'vcenter', and 'terraform'.

repo_type.description: The type of repository containing this version.  Valid values are 'public_git' or 'enterprise_git'.

zipurl.description: URL path to zip location.  If not specified, must provide content in the body of this call.

target_version.description: The semver value for this new version, if not found in the zip url package content.

include_config.description: Add all possible configuration values to this version when importing.

is_vsi.description: Indicates that the current terraform template is used to install a VSI Image.

state.description: Should the resource be present or absent.

ibm.cloud.ibm_iam_access_group.description: This module creates, updates, or deletes a ibm_iam_access_group. By default the module will look for an existing ibm_iam_access_group.

name.description:
Assign the specified name to the access group.
This field is case-insensitive and has a limit of 100 characters.
The group name has to be unique within an account.

description.description: Assign an optional description for the access group. This field has a limit of 250 characters.

account_id.description:
Account ID of the API keys(s) to query.
If a service IAM ID is specified in iam_id then account_id must match the account of the IAM ID.
If a user IAM ID is specified in iam_id then then account_id must match the account of the Authorization token.

if_match.description:
The current revision number of the group being updated.
This can be found in the Create/Get access group response ETag header.

transaction_id.description:
An optional transaction ID can be passed to your request, which can be useful for tracking calls through multiple services by using one identifier.
The header key must be set to Transaction-Id and the value is anything that you choose.
If no transaction ID is passed in, then a random ID is generated.

show_federated.description: If show_federated is true, the group will return an is_federated value that is set to true if rules exist for the group.

force.description: If force is true, delete the group as well as its associated members and rules.

state.description: Should the resource be present or absent.

ibm.cloud.ibm_iam_access_group_info.description: This module retrieves one or more ibm_iam_access_group(s).

transaction_id.description:
An optional transaction ID can be passed to your request, which can be useful for tracking calls through multiple services by using one identifier.
The header key must be set to Transaction-Id and the value is anything that you choose.
If no transaction ID is passed in, then a random ID is generated.

show_federated.description: If show_federated is true, the group will return an is_federated value that is set to true if rules exist for the group.

ibm.cloud.ibm_iam_access_group_members.description: This module creates, updates, or deletes a ibm_iam_access_group_members. By default the module will look for an existing ibm_iam_access_group_members.

members.description: An array of member objects to add to an access group.

offset.description: The offset of the first result item to be returned.

transaction_id.description:
An optional transaction ID can be passed to your request, which can be useful for tracking calls through multiple services by using one identifier.
The header key must be set to Transaction-Id and the value is anything that you choose.
If no transaction ID is passed in, then a random ID is generated.

limit.description: Return up to this limit of results where limit is between 0 and 100.

sort.description: If verbose is true, sort the results by id, name, or email.

type.description: Filter the results by member type.

verbose.description: Return user's email and name for each user ID or the name for each service ID or trusted profile.

state.description: Should the resource be present or absent.

ibm.cloud.ibm_iam_access_group_members_i.description: This module retrieves one or more ibm_iam_access_group_members(s).

offset.description: The offset of the first result item to be returned.

transaction_id.description:
An optional transaction ID can be passed to your request, which can be useful for tracking calls through multiple services by using one identifier.
The header key must be set to Transaction-Id and the value is anything that you choose.
If no transaction ID is passed in, then a random ID is generated.

limit.description: Return up to this limit of results where limit is between 0 and 100.

sort.description: If verbose is true, sort the results by id, name, or email.

type.description: Filter the results by member type.

verbose.description: Return user's email and name for each user ID or the name for each service ID or trusted profile.

ibm.cloud.ibm_iam_access_group_rule.description: This module creates, updates, or deletes a ibm_iam_access_group_rule. By default the module will look for an existing ibm_iam_access_group_rule.

expiration.description: The number of hours that the rule lives for.

conditions.description: A list of conditions the rule must satisfy.

realm_name.description: The url of the identity provider.

if_match.description:
The current revision number of the rule being updated.
This can be found in the Get Rule response ETag header.

transaction_id.description:
An optional transaction ID can be passed to your request, which can be useful for tracking calls through multiple services by using one identifier.
The header key must be set to Transaction-Id and the value is anything that you choose.
If no transaction ID is passed in, then a random ID is generated.

state.description: Should the resource be present or absent.

ibm.cloud.ibm_iam_access_group_rule_info.description: This module retrieves one or more ibm_iam_access_group_rule(s).

transaction_id.description:
An optional transaction ID can be passed to your request, which can be useful for tracking calls through multiple services by using one identifier.
The header key must be set to Transaction-Id and the value is anything that you choose.
If no transaction ID is passed in, then a random ID is generated.

ibm.cloud.ibm_iam_access_group_rules_inf.description: This module retrieves one or more ibm_iam_access_group_rules(s).

transaction_id.description:
An optional transaction ID can be passed to your request, which can be useful for tracking calls through multiple services by using one identifier.
The header key must be set to Transaction-Id and the value is anything that you choose.
If no transaction ID is passed in, then a random ID is generated.

ibm.cloud.ibm_iam_access_groups_info.description: This module retrieves one or more ibm_iam_access_groups(s).

account_id.description:
Account ID of the API keys(s) to query.
If a service IAM ID is specified in iam_id then account_id must match the account of the IAM ID.
If a user IAM ID is specified in iam_id then then account_id must match the account of the Authorization token.

iam_id.description: Return groups for member ID (IBMid, service ID or trusted profile ID).

offset.description: The offset of the first result item to be returned.

transaction_id.description:
An optional transaction ID can be passed to your request, which can be useful for tracking calls through multiple services by using one identifier.
The header key must be set to Transaction-Id and the value is anything that you choose.
If no transaction ID is passed in, then a random ID is generated.

limit.description: Return up to this limit of results where limit is between 0 and 100.

show_federated.description: If show_federated is true, each group listed will return an is_federated value that is set to true if rules exist for the group.

sort.description: Sort the results by id, name, description, or is_federated flag.

hide_public_access.description: If hide_public_access is true, do not include the Public Access Group in the results.

ibm.cloud.ibm_iam_service_id.description: This module creates, updates, or deletes a ibm_iam_service_id. By default the module will look for an existing ibm_iam_service_id.

account_id.description: ID of the account the service ID belongs to.

apikey.description: Parameters for the API key in the Create service Id V1 REST request.

name.description:
Name of the Service Id. The name is not checked for uniqueness.
Therefore multiple names with the same value can exist. Access is done via the UUID of the Service Id.

unique_instance_crns.description: Optional list of CRNs (string array) which point to the services connected to the service ID.

description.description:
The optional description of the Service Id.
The 'description' property is only available if a description was provided during a create of a Service Id.

include_history.description: Defines if the entity history is included in the response.

if_match.description:
Version of the service ID to be updated.
Specify the version that you retrieved as entity_tag (ETag header) when reading the service ID.
This value helps identifying parallel usage of this API. Pass * to indicate to update any version available. This might result in stale updates.

entity_lock.description: Indicates if the service ID is locked for further write operations. False by default.

id.description: Unique ID of the service ID.

include_activity.description:
Defines if the entity's activity is included in the response.
Retrieving activity data is an expensive operation, so please only request this when needed.

state.description: Should the resource be present or absent.

ibm.cloud.ibm_iam_service_id_info.description: This module retrieves one or more ibm_iam_service_id(s).

include_history.description: Defines if the entity history is included in the response.

id.description: Unique ID of the service ID.

include_activity.description:
Defines if the entity's activity is included in the response.
Retrieving activity data is an expensive operation, so please only request this when needed.

ibm.cloud.ibm_iam_service_ids_info.description: This module retrieves one or more ibm_iam_service_ids(s).

include_history.description: Defines if the entity history is included in the response.

account_id.description: Account ID of the service ID(s) to query. This parameter is required (unless using a pagetoken).

name.description: Name of the service ID(s) to query. Optional.20 items per page. Valid range is 1 to 100.

pagesize.description: Optional size of a single page. Default is 20 items per page. Valid range is 1 to 100.

sort.description:
Optional sort property, valid values are name, description, created_at and modified_at.
If specified, the items are sorted by the value of this property.

id.description: Unique ID of the service ID.

pagetoken.description: Optional Prev or Next page token returned from a previous query execution. Default is start with first page.

include_activity.description:
Defines if the entity's activity is included in the response.
Retrieving activity data is an expensive operation, so please only request this when needed.

order.description: Optional sort order, valid values are asc and desc. Default value is asc.

ibm.cloud.ibm_resource_alias.description: This module creates, updates, or deletes a ibm_resource_alias. By default the module will look for an existing ibm_resource_alias.

name.description: The name of the alias. Must be 180 characters or less and cannot include any special characters other than `(space) - . _ :`.

target.description: The CRN of target name(space) in a specific environment, for example, space in Dallas YP, CFEE instance etc.

state.description: Should the resource be present or absent.

ibm.cloud.ibm_resource_alias_info.description: This module retrieves one or more ibm_resource_alias(s).

ibm.cloud.ibm_resource_aliases_info.description: This module retrieves one or more ibm_resource_aliases(s).

limit.description: Limit on how many items should be returned.

start.description:
An optional token that indicates the beginning of the page of results to be returned.
Any additional query parameters are ignored if a page token is present.
If omitted, the first page of results is returned. This value is obtained from the 'next_url' field of the operation response.

ibm.cloud.ibm_resource_binding.description: This module creates, updates, or deletes a ibm_resource_binding. By default the module will look for an existing ibm_resource_binding.

role.description: The service or custom role name or it's CRN.

name.description:
The name of the binding.
Must be 180 characters or less and cannot include any special characters other than `(space) - . _ :`.

parameters.description:
Configuration options represented as key-value pairs.
Service defined options are passed through to the target resource brokers, whereas platform defined options are not.

target.description: The CRN of application to bind to in a specific environment, for example, Dallas YP, CFEE instance.

state.description: Should the resource be present or absent.

ibm.cloud.ibm_resource_binding_info.description: This module retrieves one or more ibm_resource_binding(s).

ibm.cloud.ibm_resource_bindings_info.description: This module retrieves one or more ibm_resource_bindings(s).

resource_group_id.description: The ID of the resource group.

name.description: The human-readable name of the binding.

limit.description: Limit on how many items should be returned.

start.description:
An optional token that indicates the beginning of the page of results to be returned.
Any additional query parameters are ignored if a page token is present.
If omitted, the first page of results is returned. This value is obtained from the 'next_url' field of the operation response.

resource_id.description: The unique ID of the offering (service name). This value is provided by and stored in the global catalog.

region_binding_id.description: The ID of the binding in the target environment. For example, `service_binding_id` in a given IBM Cloud environment.

ibm.cloud.ibm_resource_group.description: This module creates, updates, or deletes a C(resource_group) resource for Resource Manager.

account_id.description: The account id of the resource group.

name.description: The new name of the resource group.

resource_group_state.description: The state of the resource group.

id.description: The short or long ID of the alias.

state.description: Should the resource be present or absent.

ibm.cloud.ibm_resource_group_info.description: This module retrieves one or more C(resource_group) for Resource Manager.

id.description: The short or long ID of the alias.

ibm.cloud.ibm_resource_groups_info.description: This module retrieves one or more C(resource_groups) for Resource Manager.

date.description: The date in the format of YYYY-MM which returns resource groups. Deleted resource groups will be excluded before this month.

default.description: Boolean value to specify whether or not to list default resource groups.

account_id.description: The ID of the account that contains the resource groups that you want to get.

name.description: The name of the resource group.

include_deleted.description: Boolean value to specify whether or not to list deleted resource groups.

ibm.cloud.ibm_resource_instance.description: This module creates, updates, or deletes a ibm_resource_instance. By default the module will look for an existing ibm_resource_instance.

resource_group.description: The ID of the resource group.

plan.description: The unique ID of the plan associated with the offering. This value is provided by and stored in the global catalog.

allow_cleanup.description: A boolean that dictates if the resource instance should be deleted (cleaned up) during the processing of a region instance delete call.

name.description: The name of the instance. Must be 180 characters or less and cannot include any special characters other than `(space) - . _ :`.

parameters.description: Configuration options represented as key-value pairs that are passed through to the target resource brokers.

location.description: The deployment location where the instance should be hosted.

tags.description: Tags that are attached to the instance after provisioning. These tags can be searched and managed through the Tagging API in IBM Cloud.

entity_lock.description:
Indicates if the resource instance is locked for further update or delete operations.
It does not affect actions performed on child resources like aliases, bindings or keys. False by default.

recursive.description: Will delete resource bindings, keys and aliases associated with the instance.

state.description: Should the resource be present or absent.

ibm.cloud.ibm_resource_instance_info.description: This module retrieves one or more ibm_resource_instance(s).

ibm.cloud.ibm_resource_instances_info.description: This module retrieves one or more ibm_resource_instances(s).

resource_group_id.description: The ID of the resource group.

plan.description: The unique ID of the plan associated with the offering. This value is provided by and stored in the global catalog.

sub_type.description: The sub-type of instance, for example, `kms`.

name.description: The human-readable name of the instance.

limit.description: Limit on how many items should be returned.

start.description:
An optional token that indicates the beginning of the page of results to be returned.
Any additional query parameters are ignored if a page token is present.
If omitted, the first page of results is returned. This value is obtained from the 'next_url' field of the operation response.

service.description: The unique ID of the offering. This value is provided by and stored in the global catalog.

state_.description: The state of the instance. If not specified, instances in state `active` and `provisioning` are returned.

type.description: The type of the instance, for example, `service_instance`.

ibm.cloud.ibm_resource_key.description: This module creates, updates, or deletes a ibm_resource_key. By default the module will look for an existing ibm_resource_key.

role.description: The service or custom role name or it's CRN.

source.description: The ID of resource instance or alias.

parameters.description:
Configuration options represented as key-value pairs.
Service defined options are passed through to the target resource brokers, whereas platform defined options are not.

state.description: Should the resource be present or absent.

ibm.cloud.ibm_resource_key_info.description: This module retrieves one or more ibm_resource_key(s).

ibm.cloud.ibm_resource_keys_info.description: This module retrieves one or more ibm_resource_keys(s).

resource_group_id.description: The ID of the resource group.

name.description: The human-readable name of the key.

limit.description: Limit on how many items should be returned.

start.description:
An optional token that indicates the beginning of the page of results to be returned.
Any additional query parameters are ignored if a page token is present.
If omitted, the first page of results is returned. This value is obtained from the 'next_url' field of the operation response.

resource_id.description: The unique ID of the offering. This value is provided by and stored in the global catalog.

ibm.cloud.ibm_resource_quota_info.description: This module retrieves one or more C(resource_quota) for Resource Manager.

ibm.cloud.ibm_resource_quotas_info.description: This module retrieves one or more C(resource_quotas) for Resource Manager.

ibm.cloud.ibm_resource_reclamations_info.description: This module retrieves one or more ibm_resource_reclamations(s).

account_id.description: An alpha-numeric value identifying the account ID.

resource_instance_id.description: The ID of the resource instance.

ibm.cloud.ibm_schematics_action.short_description: Manage C(schematics_actions) for Schematics Service API.
ibm.cloud.ibm_schematics_action.description: This module creates, updates, or deletes a C(schematics_action) resource for Schematics Service API.

source_readme_url.description: URL of the C(README) file, for the source URL.

bastion_credential.description: User editable credential variable data and system generated reference to the value.

source_type.description: Type of source for the Template.

source.description: Source of templates, playbooks, or controls.

inventory.description: Target inventory record ID, used by the action or ansible playbook.

user_state.description: User defined status of the Schematics object.

bastion_connection_type.description:
Type of connection to be used when connecting to bastion host.
If the C(inventoryI(connection)type=winrm), then C(bastionI(connection)type) is not supported.

inventory_connection_type.description:
Type of connection to be used when connecting to remote host.
B(Note) Currently, WinRM supports only Windows system with the public IPs and do not support Bastion host.

resource_group.description: Resource-group name for an action. By default, an action is created in C(Default) resource group.

targets_ini.description:
Inventory of host and host group for the playbook in C(INI) file format.
For example, C("targetsI(ini": "[webserverhost] 72.22.192.6 [dbhost]172.22.192.5").
For more information, about an inventory host group syntax,
see [Inventory host groups](https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/schematics?topic=schematics-schematics-cli-reference#schematics-inventory-host-grps).

name.description:
The unique name of your action.
The name can be up to 128 characters long and can include alphanumeric characters, spaces, dashes, and underscores.
B(Example) you can use the name to stop action.

location.description:
List of locations supported by IBM Cloud Schematics service.
While creating your workspace or action, choose the right region, since it cannot be changed.
Note, this does not limit the location of the IBM Cloud resources, provisioned using Schematics.

action_id.description: Action Id.  Use GET /actions API to look up the Action Ids in your IBM Cloud account.

profile.description: Level of details returned by the get method.

propagate.description: Auto propagate the chaange or deletion to the dependent resources.

force.description: Equivalent to -force options in the command line.

x_github_token.description: The personal access token to authenticate with your private GitHub or GitLab repository and access your Terraform template.

state.description: Should the resource be present or absent.

ibm.cloud.ibm_schematics_action_info.short_description: Manage C(schematics_action) for Schematics Service API.
ibm.cloud.ibm_schematics_action_info.description: This module retrieves one or more C(schematics_action) for Schematics Service API.

action_id.description: Action Id.  Use GET /actions API to look up the Action Ids in your IBM Cloud account.

profile.description: Level of details returned by the get method.

ibm.cloud.ibm_schematics_inventory.short_description: Manage C(schematics_inventorys) for Schematics Service API.
ibm.cloud.ibm_schematics_inventory.description: This module creates, updates, or deletes a C(schematics_inventory) resource for Schematics Service API.

inventories_ini.description: Input inventory of host and host group for the playbook, in the C(.ini) file format.

resource_group.description: Resource-group name for the Inventory definition.   By default, Inventory definition will be created in Default Resource Group.

resource_queries.description: Input resource query definitions that is used to dynamically generate the inventory of host and host group for the playbook.

name.description:
The unique name of your Inventory definition.
The name can be up to 128 characters long and can include alphanumeric characters, spaces, dashes, and underscores.

description.description: The description of your Inventory definition. The description can be up to 2048 characters long in size.

location.description:
List of locations supported by IBM Cloud Schematics service.
While creating your workspace or action, choose the right region, since it cannot be changed.
Note, this does not limit the location of the IBM Cloud resources, provisioned using Schematics.

inventory_id.description:
Resource Inventory Id.
Use C(GET /v2/inventories) API to look up the Resource Inventory definition Ids  in your IBM Cloud account.

profile.description: Level of details returned by the get method.

propagate.description: Auto propagate the chaange or deletion to the dependent resources.

force.description: Equivalent to -force options in the command line.

state.description: Should the resource be present or absent.

ibm.cloud.ibm_schematics_inventory_info.short_description: Manage C(schematics_inventory) for Schematics Service API.
ibm.cloud.ibm_schematics_inventory_info.description: This module retrieves one or more C(schematics_inventory) for Schematics Service API.

inventory_id.description: Resource Inventory Id.  Use C(GET /v2/inventories) API to look up the Resource Inventory definition Ids  in your IBM Cloud account.

profile.description: Level of details returned by the get method.

ibm.cloud.ibm_schematics_job.short_description: Manage C(schematics_jobs) for Schematics Service API.
ibm.cloud.ibm_schematics_job.description: This module creates, updates, or deletes a C(schematics_job) resource for Schematics Service API.

settings.description: Environment variables used by the Job while performing Action or Workspace.

inputs.description: Job inputs used by Action or Workspace.

command_object.description: Name of the Schematics automation resource.

tags.description: User defined tags, while running the job.

command_options.description: Command line options for the command.

location.description:
List of locations supported by IBM Cloud Schematics service.
While creating your workspace or action, choose the right region, since it cannot be changed.
Note, this does not limit the location of the IBM Cloud resources, provisioned using Schematics.

refresh_token.description:
The IAM refresh token for the user or service identity.
B(Retrieving refresh token) I( Use C(export IBMCLOUDI(API)KEY=<ibmcloudI(api)key>),and execute
C(curl -X POST "https://iam.cloud.ibm.com/identity/token" -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded"
-d "grantI(type=urn:ibm:params:oauth:grant-type:apikey&apikey=$IBMCLOUD)APII(KEY" -u bx:bx).)
For more information, about creating IAM access token and API Docs, refer,
[IAM access token](/apidocs/iam-identity-token-api#gettoken-password) and [Create API key](/apidocs/iam-identity-token-api#create-api-key).
B(Limitation): I( If the token is expired, you can use C(refresh token) to get a new IAM access token.)
The C(refresh token) parameter cannot be used to retrieve a new IAM access token.
I( When the IAM access token is about to expire, use the API key to create a new access token.)

job_id.description: Job Id. Use C(GET /v2/jobs) API to look up the Job Ids in your IBM Cloud account.

t_id.description:
The ID of the Terraform template for which you want to retrieve the Terraform statefile.
When you create a workspace, the Terraform template that your workspace points to is assigned a unique ID.
To find this ID, use the C(GET /v1/workspaces) API and review the templateI(data.id value.

w_id.description: The ID of the workspace for which you want to retrieve the Terraform statefile.  To find the workspace ID, use the C(GET /v1/workspaces) API.

ibm.cloud.ibm_schematics_workspace.short_description: Manage C(schematics_workspaces) for Schematics Service API.
ibm.cloud.ibm_schematics_workspace.description: This module creates, updates, or deletes a C(schematics_workspace) resource for Schematics Service API.

agent_id.description: agent id which is binded to with the workspace.

applied_shareddata_ids.description: List of applied shared dataset ID.

shared_data.description:
Information about the Target used by the templates originating from the IBM Cloud catalog offerings.
This information is not relevant for workspace created using your own Terraform template.

workspace_status_update_request_workspac.description: Input to update the workspace status.

tags.description: A list of tags that are associated with the workspace.

catalog_ref.description: Information about the software template that you chose from the IBM Cloud catalog. This information is returned for IBM Cloud catalog offerings only.

resource_group.description: The ID of the resource group where you want to provision the workspace.

template_repo.description: Input variables for the Template repoository, while creating a workspace.

workspace_status_msg.description: Information about the last job that ran against the workspace. -.

template_repo_update_request_template_re.description: Input to update the template repository data.

name.description:
The name of your workspace. The name can be up to 128 characters long and can include alphanumeric characters, spaces, dashes, and underscores.
When you create a workspace for your own Terraform template,
consider including the microservice component that you set up with your Terraform template and
the IBM Cloud environment where you want to deploy your resources in your name.

location.description:
The location where you want to create your Schematics workspace and run the Schematics jobs.
The location that you enter must match the API endpoint that you use.
For example, if you use the Frankfurt API endpoint, you must specify C(eu-de) as your location.
If you use an API endpoint for a geography and you do not specify a location, Schematics determines the location based on availability.

refresh_token.description:
The IAM refresh token for the user or service identity.
The IAM refresh token is required only if you want to destroy the Terraform resources before deleting the Schematics workspace.
If you want to delete the workspace only and keep all your Terraform resources, refresh token is not required.
B(Retrieving refresh token)I( Use C(export IBMCLOUDI(API)KEY=<ibmcloudI(api)key>),
and execute C(curl -X POST "https://iam.cloud.ibm.com/identity/token" -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded"
-d "grantI(type=urn:ibm:params:oauth:grant-type:apikey&apikey=$IBMCLOUD)APII(KEY" -u bx:bx).)
For more information, about creating IAM access token and API Docs, refer, [IAM access token](/apidocs/iam-identity-token-api#gettoken-password)
and [Create API key](/apidocs/iam-identity-token-api#create-api-key).
B(Limitation) I( If the token is expired, you can use C(refresh token) to get a new IAM access token.)
The C(refresh)token) parameter cannot be used to retrieve a new IAM access token.
I( When the IAM access token is about to expire, use the API key to create a new access token.)

w_id.description: The ID of the workspace.  To find the workspace ID, use the C(GET /v1/workspaces) API.

x_github_token.description: The personal access token to authenticate with your private GitHub or GitLab repository and access your Terraform template.

destroy_resources.description:
If set to C(true), refreshI(token header configuration is required to delete all the Terraform resources, and the Schematics workspace.
If set to C(false), you can remove only the workspace.
Your Terraform resources are still available and must be managed with the resource dashboard or CLI.

state.description: Should the resource be present or absent.

activity_id.description: The ID of the activity or job, for which you want to retrieve details.  To find the job ID, use the C(GET /v1/workspaces/{id}/actions) API.
